Lemma 20.3Evaluation formula

For every boundary matrix \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and every length-\(L\) word \(\sigma \),

\[ \Gamma _{L}(X)(\sigma ) = \operatorname{tr}(A^{\sigma } X). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.32Ground-space map injectivity for block-injective tensors

If \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective, then \(\Gamma _{L_0}\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.7Nontriviality criterion

If \(d^L {\gt} D^2\), then \(G_L(A)\) is a proper subspace of the local Hilbert space:

\[ G_L(A) \neq (\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L \to \mathbb {C}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.52Local symmetry implies string order

Assume \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda )=1\) and \(\mathcal{E}_A(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\). If \(u\) is a local symmetry of \(A\) with respect to \(\Lambda \), then string order exists for \(u\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.127Infimum attainment for SL-filterings

For a positive-definite Choi matrix \(\tau \), the infimum of \(\operatorname{tr}\! \big[(S_{2} \otimes _{k} S_{1}) \tau (S_{2} \otimes _{k} S_{1})^{\dagger }\big]\) over \(S_{1}, S_{2} \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with \(\det S_{1} = \det S_{2} = 1\) is attained. Not yet proved—requires compactness of bounded subsets of \(\operatorname{SL}(D, \mathbb {C})\) and the extreme-value theorem.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.26Block injectivity and blocked-tensor injectivity

A tensor \(A\) is \(L\)-block injective if and only if its \(L\)-blocked tensor \(A^{[L]}\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.72Peripheral eigenvectors are invertible

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital Kraus map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point. If \(X \ne 0\) satisfies \(E(X) = \mu X\) with \(|\mu | = 1\), then \(X\) is invertible.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.145Periodic block decomposition: forward inclusion

Assume \(\mu _{j} \neq 0\) for every block index \(j\). The supremum of the blockwise periodic-chain ground spaces is contained in the assembled tensor’s periodic-chain ground space:

\[ \bigsqcup _{j} \operatorname {ChainGround}_{N,L}(A_{j}) \subseteq \operatorname {ChainGround}_{N,L}(\operatorname {Assemble}(\mu , A)), \]

where \(j\) ranges over all block indices.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.146Forward inclusion for assembled parent ground spaces

For a canonical-form/BNT family, the supremum of the blockwise periodic-chain ground spaces is contained in the assembled parent ground space:

\[ \bigsqcup _{j} \operatorname {ChainGround}_{N,L}(A_{j}) \subseteq \operatorname {ParentGround}_{N,L}(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.29Kraus operators lie in the one-step span

Every Kraus operator \(A^i\) belongs to the one-step span \(S_1(A)\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.155Left multiplication identity for sector words

If a projection commutes with all Kraus operators, then word evaluation for the left sector tensor equals left multiplication by the projection.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.156Sector support

The left sector tensor is supported on the chosen projection.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.42Left-tail compatibility

Assume \(\psi \) on \(K+L_0+1\) sites satisfies the left ground condition on the first \(K+L_0\) sites and the suffix ground condition on every fixed prefix of length \(K\). Then there exist matrices \((Z_j)_j\) and \((Y_u)_u\) such that

\[ \psi (\sigma ,j) = \operatorname{tr}(A^\sigma Z_j), \qquad \psi (u,\tau ) = \operatorname{tr}(A^\tau Y_u), \]

and for all \(j\) and \(u\),

\[ Z_j A^u = A_j Y_u. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 4.10Promotion to algebra homomorphism

If \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is a multiplicative surjective \(\mathbb {C}\)-linear map, then \(T(\mathbb {1}) = \mathbb {1}\). Equivalently, the same underlying function defines a \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra homomorphism.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.20Local term annihilates the MPV

For each site \(i\), \(h_i\, V^{(N)}(A) = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.108Cyclic averaging for the transported ES local terms

The transported parent Hamiltonian is the sum of the transported local terms \(H_{N,\mathrm{ES}} = \sum _i h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}\), and each transported local term admits the exact cyclic-averaging formula

\[ h_{i,\mathrm{ES}} = d^{-L} \sum _{\tau } R_{i,\tau }^{\dagger } P_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A) R_{i,\tau }. \]

Lemma 20.112Local-term kernel via cyclic restrictions

For \(L\le N\), a transported local term \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}\) annihilates a vector \(v\) iff every boundary-filled restriction of \(v\) to the cyclic \(L\)-window starting at \(i\) lies in \(G_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\).

Lemma 9.47Lower-triangular Kraus condition implies corner invariance

Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor and let \(P\) be an orthogonal projection. If, for every physical index \(i\),

\[ (\mathbb {1}- P) A^i P = 0 \]

then

\[ P \, \mathcal{E}_A(P X P) \, P = \mathcal{E}_A(P X P) \]

for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.136Martingale condition for MPS

Let \(A\) be injective and fix an interaction range \(L \ge 2\) as in Definition 20.10. Let \(h_L(A) := \Pi _{G_L(A)^\perp }\) be the \(L\)-site parent interaction, and for each chain length and site \(i\) let \(h_i\) denote its translate as in Definition 20.16. Then for every pair of overlapping terms \(h_i\) and \(h_j\) with \(0 {\lt} d_N(i,j) {\lt} L\), where \(d_N(i,j) := \min (|i-j|,\, N-|i-j|)\) is the cyclic distance on the ring (i.e. whose \(L\)-site supports share at least one site under periodic boundary conditions), the martingale condition of Theorem 20.135 holds with constants \(c_{ij}\) and \(\gamma {\gt} 0\) depending only on \(A\) (not on \(N\)). Note: The proof below fully establishes the martingale constants only for \(L = 2\); for \(L {\gt} 2\) the existence of suitable constants is asserted without complete proof (see the remark at the end of the proof). Only the \(L = 2\) case is used in the spectral-gap theorem below. (When \(L = 1\) the interaction terms have disjoint supports, so there are no overlapping pairs and the spectral gap follows directly without the martingale argument.)

LaTeX
Lemma 20.9Membership in the transported local ground space

A Euclidean vector lies in \(G_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\) exactly when transporting it back through the canonical coefficient-space equivalence gives an element of the original local ground space \(G_L(A)\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.105Membership in the transported parent ground space

A vector belongs to the transported parent ground space exactly when the transported parent Hamiltonian annihilates it.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.49Mirror wrapped-window one-sided compatibility

Assume \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block injective with \(L_0{\gt}0\) and \(M \ge L_0\). If the opposite reduced wrapped window has boundary matrices \(Y_\tau \), then

\[ X A^j C^-_\tau = A^j Y_\tau \]

for every physical letter \(j\), where \(C^-_\tau \) is the complementary word seen from the opposite boundary-crossing position.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 3.62Reverse inclusion of adjoint fixed points

Every adjoint fixed point of the unblocked transfer map \(E_1\) is an adjoint fixed point of every blocked transfer map \(E_n\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 3.7Identification with the doubled-index MPS transfer map

The transfer map of an MPO tensor agrees with the transfer map of its doubled-index MPS view.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.39Blocking preserves MPV coefficients

For each basis state \(\sigma = (\sigma _1, \ldots , \sigma _N)\) of the blocked system, where each \(\sigma _k\) is an \(L\)-tuple in \(\{ 0, \ldots , d{-}1\} ^L\), there exists a basis state \(\widetilde{\sigma } = (\widetilde{\sigma }_1, \ldots , \widetilde{\sigma }_{NL})\) of the original system such that

\[ V^{(N)}(A^{[L]})_\sigma \; =\; V^{(NL)}(A)_{\widetilde{\sigma }}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.33Common phase-cover data give nonzero-block span equality

A common MPV phase cover for two nonzero-weight block families gives equality of their finite-length MPV spans at every system size.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.56The MPV is the ground-space map at the identity

For every chain length \(N\),

\[ V^{(N)}(A) = \Gamma _{N}(\mathbb {1}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.61The MPV satisfies every cyclic window constraint

If \(0 {\lt} N\) and \(L \le N\), then

\[ V^{(N)}(A) \in \mathcal G_{N,L}(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.57MPV lies in the ground space

\(V^{(N)}(A) \in G_N(A)\) for every \(N\), with boundary matrix \(X = I\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.17Scaling of MPV components

Let \(\zeta A\) denote the tensor with matrices \(\{ \zeta \, A^i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}\). Then for every \(N\) and every configuration \(\sigma = (i_1, \ldots , i_N)\),

\[ V^{(N)}(\zeta A)_\sigma \; =\; \zeta ^{N} \, V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.19MPV window membership

For any window of \(L\) consecutive sites starting at position \(i\) in an \(N\)-site periodic chain (\(L \le N\)), the restriction of the MPV state to that window lies in \(G_L(A)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.102Naimark square roots satisfy the Kraus normalization

For a POVM \(\{ E_i\} \) with square roots \(E_i = M_i^\dagger M_i\), one has

\[ \sum _i M_i^\dagger M_i = \mathbb {1}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.32Common MPV phase cover gives nonzero-block span equality

Let two finite nonzero-weight block families map onto a third finite family of common blocks. Suppose both maps are surjective, and each block has finite-length MPV vectors equal to a nonzero scalar power times the MPV vectors of its common block. Then, for every system size, the two nonzero-weight block families span the same MPV subspace.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.13Norm of embedded matrix

\(\| \mathrm{vec}(X)\| ^2 = \| X\| _F^2\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.35String order prevents decay to zero

If \(A\) has string order for \(u\) with respect to a boundary state \(\Lambda \), then for some boundary matrices \(X,Y\) the sequence \(R_L(u;X,Y)\) does not tend to \(0\) as \(L \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.6Ambient-space dimension

The coefficient-function model satisfies

\[ \dim \! (\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L \to \mathbb {C})=d^L. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.25One-site injectivity gives one-block injectivity

If the one-site matrices \(\{ A^i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}\) span \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), then the length-one word products span \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), so \(A\) is \(1\)-block injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.31Normality and Kraus rank under one-step augmentation

Let \(X \in S_1(A)\), and let \(\widetilde A\) be the one-step augmentation of \(A\) by \(X\). If \(A\) is normal, then \(\widetilde A\) is normal, and

\[ \operatorname{kr}(\widetilde A)=\operatorname{kr}(A). \]

Lemma 10.30Redundant one-step augmentation

Let \(X \in S_1(A)\), and let \(\widetilde A\) be the one-step augmentation of \(A\) by \(X\). Then

\[ S_n(\widetilde A)=S_n(A) \]

for every \(n \ge 0\).

Lemma 7.50Compression auxiliary for Jensen

Let \(Y\) be positive definite and let \(W\) be an isometry. Then

\[ (W^\ast Y W)^{-1} \le W^\ast Y^{-1} W. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.51Finite-POVM compression identities

If the defect block satisfies

\[ S S^\ast = \mathbb {1}- \sum _{i \in \iota } C_i C_i^\ast , \]

then the dilation is an isometry, compressing the weighted scalar block-diagonal matrix gives

\[ \sum _{i \in \iota } w_i\, C_i C_i^\ast + t\, S S^\ast , \]

the defect relation rewrites as

\[ \sum _{i \in \iota } C_i C_i^\ast + S S^\ast = \mathbb {1}, \]

and strict positivity of all weights together with \(t{\gt}0\) implies positive definiteness of the scalar block-diagonal matrix. If all weights are nonzero and the defect scalar is nonzero, the inverse diagonal has reciprocal weights, and compressing this inverse gives

\[ \sum _{i \in \iota } w_i^{-1}\, C_i C_i^\ast + t^{-1}\, S S^\ast . \]
LaTeX

  • TNLean.OperatorJensen.povmDiagonal_inv
  • TNLean.OperatorJensen.povmIsometry_compress_diagonal_inv
Lemma 7.52Finite-POVM resolvent inequality

Let \(w_i\ge 0\) and \(t{\gt}0\). If the defect block satisfies

\[ S S^\ast = \mathbb {1}- \sum _{i \in \iota } C_i C_i^\ast , \]

then

\[ \left(\sum _{i \in \iota } w_i\, C_i C_i^\ast + t\, \mathbb {1}\right)^{-1} \le \sum _{i \in \iota } (w_i+t)^{-1}\, C_i C_i^\ast + t^{-1}\, S S^\ast . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.105Orbit iterate preserves orthogonal projection

Under the same cyclic shift and multiplicative-domain hypotheses as Lemma 11.104, suppose additionally that \(T\) maps every orthogonal projection supported on a single sector to an orthogonal projection. If \(Q\) is an orthogonal projection supported on \(P_k\), then \(T^l(Q)\) is an orthogonal projection for each \(0 \le l {\lt} m\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.104Orbit iterate supported on shifted sector

Let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be orthogonal projections with cyclic shift \(T(P_{k+1}) = P_k\), and suppose \(T\) is multiplicative on each sector corner. If \(Q\) is supported on sector \(P_k\) (i.e. \(QP_k = Q = P_k Q\)), then for each \(0 \le l {\lt} m\) the iterate \(T^l(Q)\) is supported on the shifted sector \(P_{k-l}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.103Orbit-sum fixed point

Let \(T\) be a linear endomorphism and \(Q\) a matrix satisfying \(T^m(Q) = Q\). Then the orbit sum \(\sum _{l=0}^{m-1} T^l(Q)\) is a fixed point of \(T\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.106Orbit sum of a full sector projection

Let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be orthogonal projections with \(\sum _k P_k = \mathbb {1}\) and cyclic shift \(T(P_{k+1}) = P_k\). Then for every sector index \(k\),

\[ \sum _{l=0}^{m-1} T^l(P_k) \; =\; \mathbb {1}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.114Orbit-sum construction of the sector lift

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor, \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) a cyclic family of orthogonal sector projections with \(\sum _k P_k = \mathbb {1}\), and set \(T = \mathcal{E}_A^\dagger \). Assume two auxiliary facts, both discharged from the block-diagonal canonical-form analysis of [ DlCCSPG17 ] :

  1. (Projection step.) For each sector index \(k\) and each orthogonal projection \(X\) supported on \(P_k\), the image \(T(X)\) is again an orthogonal projection.

  2. (Fixed-point upgrade.) For each sector index \(k\) and each orthogonal subprojection \(Q \le P_k\), if \(Q\) is a corner fixed point of \(T^m\) then \(T^m(Q) = Q\).

Then for every sector \(k\) and every orthogonal subprojection \(Q \le P_k\) which is a corner fixed point of \(T^m\), the orbit sum

\[ R \; :=\; \sum _{l=0}^{m-1} T^l(Q) \]

is an orthogonal projection, preserved by \(T\), with \(Q = 0 \Leftrightarrow R = 0\) and \(Q = P_k \Leftrightarrow R = \mathbb {1}\). In particular \(R\) witnesses the orbit-sum lift hypothesis of Theorem 11.107.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.116Orbit-sum construction, fixed-point-algebra variant

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor with irreducible adjoint transfer map, and let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be cyclic orthogonal sector projections. Assume the single fixed-point-algebra rigidity hypothesis from Definition 11.109. Then the orbit-sum lift holds: for every orthogonal subprojection \(Q \le P_k\) whose corner is preserved by \((\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger )^m\), there is a global projection \(R\) invariant under \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger \) with the same zero/full-sector tests.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.115Orbit-sum construction, projection-step variant

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor with irreducible adjoint transfer map, and let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be cyclic orthogonal sector projections. Assume the projection-step hypothesis of Lemma 11.114. Then the orbit-sum lift holds: for every orthogonal subprojection \(Q \le P_k\) whose corner is preserved by \((\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger )^m\), there is a global projection \(R\) invariant under \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger \) with the same zero/full-sector tests.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.118Ordered projection row-sum quadratic-form reduction

Let \(P_i\) be a finite family of symmetric projections and let \(H=\sum _i P_i\). If the ordered off-diagonal forms obey

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle P_i v, P_j v\rangle \ge -(1-\gamma )c_{ij}\operatorname {Re}\langle P_i v,v\rangle \qquad (i\ne j), \]

with row sums \(\sum _{j\ne i} c_{ij}\le 1\) and \(\gamma \le 1\), then \(H^2\ge \gamma H\) as a quadratic form.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.44Overlap equals conjugate inner product

\(O_{AB}(N) = \overline{\langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle }\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.45Overlap determined by positive-length MPV

If \(V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(A')_\sigma \) and \(V^{(N)}(B)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(B')_\sigma \) for all \(N {\gt} 0\) and all \(\sigma \), then \(O_{AB}(N) = O_{A'B'}(N)\) for all positive \(N\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.67Padded complement annihilation

If \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective and \(q \ge 1\), then an operator \(Z\) that annihilates every length-\(k\) word product on the left is zero whenever \(k \le qL_0\):

\[ Z A^w = 0 \quad (|w|=k) \qquad \Longrightarrow \qquad Z=0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.153Pair algebra projections are full

If one of the two blocks is injective, then the corresponding coordinate projection of the pair algebra generated by the simultaneous one-letter pairs is the full matrix algebra. Moreover, every finite pair word lies in this pair algebra, and the all-word linear span is the submodule generated by the pair algebra. If the pair algebra is the graph of an algebra automorphism of the matrix algebra, Skolem–Noether turns this graph case into gauge-phase equivalence, so under non-gauge-equivalence the graph branch is impossible. For two blocks of unequal bond dimensions, the graph branch is also impossible because it would identify matrix algebras with different finite dimensions over \(\mathbb {C}\).

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.pairTraceSeparatingAll_of_injective_dim_ne
Lemma 20.154Conditional padding from exact homogeneous identity witnesses

Homogeneous identity-padding witnesses multiply by concatenation of words. Therefore, if one has a positive period and a full residue window of homogeneous pair spans, then the simultaneous identity pair lies in all sufficiently large homogeneous pair spans. Combined with all-length pair trace separation, this period-window input gives one homogeneous pair trace-separation length. This does not prove the David–Perez-Garcia direct-sum input by itself: fullness of spans up to a bounded length is weaker than the exact homogeneous input used here.

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.pairIdentity_mem_pairWordTupleSpan_add

  • MPSTensor.exists_pairTraceSeparatingAt_of_not_gaugePhaseEquiv_of_pairWordTupleSpanTop_period_window
  • MPSTensor.exists_pairTraceSeparatingAt_of_injective_dim_ne_of_pairWordTupleSpanTop_period_window
Lemma 20.155Pair trace separation gives pairwise selectors

At a fixed length \(S\), the pair trace-separation criterion is dual to full pair product-span. Therefore a pair trace-separation witness supplies a word polynomial that is identity on the first block and zero on the second. A common witness for all ordered distinct pairs gives pairwise block-separating words at the same length.

Lemma 11.101Pairwise orthogonality from projection sum

Let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be orthogonal projections summing to the identity: \(\sum _k P_k = \mathbb {1}\). Then for \(i \neq j\) we have \(P_i P_j = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.10Sector decomposition formula

For a sector decomposition \(S\) with associated tensor \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\), the MPV satisfies

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{j=1}^{g} c_{S,j}^{(N)} V^{(N)}(A_j)_\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.21Parent Hamiltonian annihilates the MPV

For every \(N\), the MPV state satisfies

\[ H_N(A,L)\, V^{(N)}(A) = 0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.132Parent-Hamiltonian gap from overlapping cyclic Friedrichs data

Fix \(L{\gt}1\) and let \(i\sim _L j\) mean that the cyclic supports of the length-\(L\) windows at \(i\) and \(j\) meet. Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that overlapping off-diagonal terms satisfy

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -(1-\tfrac {1}{4L})\frac{1}{2(L-1)} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle . \]

Then the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.134Parent-Hamiltonian gap from a cyclic overlap compression constant

Fix \(L{\gt}1\), and let \(m=2(L-1)\). Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair that

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le \eta \, \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

If \(\gamma {\gt}0\), \(\gamma \le 1\), and \(\eta \le (1-\gamma )/m\), then \(0{\lt}\gamma \) and, for every admissible \(N\) and every \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ \gamma \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]

The assertion \(0{\lt}\gamma \) repeats the hypothesis, in the same shape as the strict statement below. In particular, if \(\eta \ge 0\) and \(\eta m{\lt}1\), then \(0{\lt}1-\eta m\) and, for every admissible \(N\) and every \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ (1-\eta m)\| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]
LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_cyclic_window_overlap_norm_bound_of_le
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_cyclic_window_overlap_norm_bound_of_lt
Lemma 20.133Parent-Hamiltonian gap from overlapping cyclic norm-compression data

Fix \(L{\gt}1\). Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair that

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le (1-\tfrac {1}{4L})\frac{1}{2(L-1)} \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

Then the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.131Parent-Hamiltonian gap from cyclic-window Friedrichs data

Fix \(L{\gt}1\) and let \(i\sim _L j\) mean that the cyclic supports of the length-\(L\) windows at \(i\) and \(j\) meet. Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that overlapping off-diagonal terms satisfy

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -(1-\tfrac {1}{4L})\frac{1}{2(L-1)} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle . \]

Then \(1/(4L){\gt}0\), and the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.116Parent-Hamiltonian quadratic-form reduction

Let \(H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\) be the transported parent Hamiltonian. If a constant \(\gamma {\gt} 0\) satisfies the uniform quadratic-form estimate

\[ \gamma \, \operatorname {Re}\langle H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v, v\rangle \le \operatorname {Re}\langle H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v, H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\rangle \]

for every \(N \ge 2L\) and every vector \(v\), then

\[ \gamma \, \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| \]

for every \(v \in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \). In particular, with \(L{\gt}1\) and \(\gamma = 1/(4L)\), the explicit gap-bound conclusion follows once this same quadratic-form estimate is supplied.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_norm_bound_of_quadratic_form
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_quadratic_form
Lemma 20.22Frustration-freeness of the MPV

The MPV state is frustration-free for the parent model.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.130Parent-Hamiltonian gap from finite-overlap Friedrichs data

Fix \(L{\gt}1\) and set \(m=2(L-1)\). Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that the hypotheses of the fixed-chain finite-overlap Friedrichs reduction hold with \(\gamma =1/(4L)\) and this value of \(m\). Then \(1/(4L){\gt}0\), and the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.123Parent-Hamiltonian finite-overlap Friedrichs quadratic reduction

For a fixed chain length \(N\), let \(m\) be a positive integer and let \(\gamma \le 1\). Suppose an overlap predicate on local windows has at most \(m\) overlapping off-diagonal windows in each row. If non-overlapping local terms have nonnegative ordered cross terms, overlapping local terms satisfy the finite-overlap Friedrichs estimate

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -(1-\gamma )\frac{1}{m} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle , \]

and the transported local terms are symmetric projections, then the transported Hamiltonian satisfies \(H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}{}^2\ge \gamma H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}\) as a quadratic form.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.124Finite-overlap norm-compression reduction

For a fixed chain length \(N\), let \(m{\gt}0\) and \(\gamma \le 1\). Suppose an overlap predicate on local windows has at most \(m\) overlapping off-diagonal windows in each row, non-overlapping local terms have nonnegative ordered cross terms, and overlapping local terms satisfy

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le (1-\gamma )\frac{1}{m}\| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

Then \(H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}{}^2\ge \gamma H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}\) as a quadratic form. The same conclusion holds from a separate coefficient \(\eta \) satisfying \(\eta \le (1-\gamma )/m\).

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_quadratic_form_of_finite_overlap_norm_bound_of_le
Lemma 20.140Definition by assembled chain ground space

By definition, this subspace is exactly the chain ground space of the assembled tensor.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.122Parent-Hamiltonian gap from ordered local row bounds

Assume uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that the transported local terms satisfy the ordered row-sum estimate above with \(\gamma =1/(4L)\). If \(L{\gt}1\), then \(1/(4L){\gt}0\) and, for every \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ \frac{1}{4L}\| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.121Parent-Hamiltonian ordered row-sum quadratic-form reduction

For a fixed chain length \(N\), suppose the transported local terms \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}\) satisfy the ordered row-sum estimate

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}} v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -(1-\gamma )c_{ij}\operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle \qquad (i\ne j), \]

with \(\sum _{j\ne i}c_{ij}\le 1\) and \(\gamma \le 1\). Then the transported parent Hamiltonian satisfies

\[ \gamma \operatorname {Re}\langle H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle \le \operatorname {Re}\langle H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}v, H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \]

for every vector \(v\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.11Parent interaction kills ground-space elements

For any \(v \in G_L(A)\), we have \(h_L(A)\, v = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.111Kernel of the Euclidean parent interaction

The Euclidean local interaction \(P_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\) annihilates exactly the Euclidean local ground space:

\[ P_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v=0 \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad v\in G_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.33Per-block bijectivity

Each \(T_k\) is bijective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.32Per-block multiplicativity

For each block \(k\), the per-block linear extension \(T_k\) is multiplicative: \(T_k(MN) = T_k(M)\, T_k(N)\) for all \(M, N \in M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.9Equivalence structures for periodic relations

Both periodic relations are equivalence relations (reflexive, symmetric, transitive).

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.PeriodicMPSTensor.instEquivalenceSameState
  • MPSTensor.PeriodicMPSTensor.instEquivalenceGaugeEquiv
Lemma 5.45Peripheral closure via adjoint fixed point

Let \(E(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) be a Kraus map that is unital (\(\sum _i K_i K_i^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\)), and assume:

  1. the adjoint Kraus map \(E^*(X) = \sum _i K_i^\dagger X K_i\) has a positive definite fixed point \(\rho {\gt} 0\), and

  2. \(E\) is irreducible.

Then \(\mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) is closed under powers: if \(\mu \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) then \(\mu ^n \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) for every \(n \in \mathbb {N}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.73Peripheral eigenvalues: product closure

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital Kraus map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point. If \(\mu , \nu \) are peripheral eigenvalues of \(E\), then so is \(\mu \nu \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.34Powering collapses peripheral eigenvalues

Let \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a linear map, and assume that \(E\) has a nonzero fixed point \(\rho \neq 0\). Let \(p {\gt} 0\). If every peripheral eigenvalue \(\mu \) of \(E\) satisfies \(\mu ^p = 1\), then \(\mathrm{peripheral}(E^p) = \{ 1\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.29Composition of permutation twists

If \(\sigma (gh) = \sigma (g) \circ \sigma (h)\) for all \(g, h \in G\), then

\[ A^{\sigma _{gh}} = (A^{\sigma _g})^{\sigma _h}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.28Identity permutation twist

If \(\sigma (1) = \mathrm{id}\), then \((A^{\sigma _1})^i = A^i\) for all \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.6Defining property of the physical realisation map

For every \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and every physical index \(i\),

\[ A^i\, X = \sum _{j=0}^{d-1} O_A(X)_{ij}\, A^j. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.36Nondegeneracy of the product trace pairing

Let \(M = (M_0, \ldots , M_{r-1}) \in \prod _{k} M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\). If \(\sum _{k} \operatorname{tr}(M_k N_k) = 0\) for all \(N = (N_0, \ldots , N_{r-1})\), then \(M = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.37Injectivity of the product Gram map

Let \(\{ A_k\} \) be a family of injective block tensors. The product Gram map \(M \mapsto (k, i \mapsto \operatorname{tr}(M_k \cdot A_k^i))\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.20Positive-definite trace test

If \(A{\gt}0\), \(X\ge 0\), and \(\operatorname{tr}(AX)=0\), then \(X=0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.157Product-word span from BNT selector words

Let \(((\mu _j),(A_j))\) be in canonical form with BNT separation. Suppose there are length-\(S\) block-selector words: for each sector \(k\), a linear combination of length-\(S\) words evaluates to \(I\) on \(A_k\) and to \(0\) on every other block. Then the simultaneous length-\((1+S)\) block-word evaluations span \(\prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C})\), with positive length. It is enough, by Lemma 20.156, to construct pairwise separators for every ordered pair of distinct blocks; by Lemma 20.155, a common pair trace-separation length is one sufficient finite-dimensional witness. This witness remains homogeneous: either it is supplied directly as fixed-length pair separation, or it is obtained from an explicit period-window hypothesis as in Lemma 20.154. Consequently, the pulled-back words at the resulting length for \(\operatorname {Assemble}(\mu ,A)\) span every virtual sector projection, and the corresponding commutant reduction is available.

LaTeX

Lemma 20.115Quadratic form to norm bound

Let \(H\) be a positive semidefinite self-adjoint operator on a finite-dimensional complex Hilbert space, and suppose that, for all \(v\),

\[ \gamma \, \langle v, H v\rangle \le \langle H v, H v\rangle \]

i.e. \(H^2 \ge \gamma H\) as a quadratic form, for some \(\gamma {\gt} 0\). Then for every \(v\) in the orthogonal complement of \(\ker H\),

\[ \gamma \, \| v\| \le \| H v\| . \]

In particular, every positive eigenvalue of \(H\) is at least \(\gamma \). The positive-semidefiniteness hypothesis is essential: without it, \(H = -\mathrm{Id}\) with \(\gamma = 2\) satisfies the quadratic-form inequality vacuously but violates the norm bound.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.36Rank-one extraction from a non-invertible eigenvector

Let \(A\) be a normal MPS tensor with bond dimension \(D \ge 1\). Suppose that a Kraus operator \(A^{i_0}\) is non-invertible and that there exists a vector \(\varphi \in \mathbb {C}^D\) and a nonzero scalar \(\mu \) such that \(A^{i_0}\varphi =\mu \varphi \). Then, for every \(\psi \in \mathbb {C}^D\),

\[ |\varphi \rangle \! \langle \psi | \in S_{D^2-D+1}(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.15Replacing an extracted window

If \(L \le N\), then replacing the cyclic window at \(i\) in \(\sigma \) by the values extracted from that same \(\sigma \) leaves \(\sigma \) unchanged:

\[ \operatorname {replaceWindow}_{L,i}(\sigma ,\operatorname {extractWindow}_{L,i}(\sigma )) = \sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.56Nonzero replicated weights with sector phases

If the replicated sector weights are additionally multiplied by nonzero sector phases, then the resulting sector weights are still nonzero.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.55Nonzero weights after sector replication

Suppose a nonzero-weight block \(k\) is replaced by a finite family of cyclic sectors. If \(\mu _k \ne 0\), then every replicated sector weight \(\mu _k^{p_k}\) remains nonzero.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.147Block-diagonal commutant reduction from span extension

Suppose the virtual sector projections \(P_j\) of a dependent direct-sum bond space lie in the span of a family of matrices. Any boundary matrix commuting with that family commutes with each \(P_j\), hence is block diagonal. Applied after pulling an assembled tensor back along the direct-sum reindexing, length-\(m\) word commutation plus the corresponding projection-span hypothesis forces all off-block entries of the pulled-back boundary matrix to vanish.

LaTeX
Lean
  • Matrix.isBlockDiagonal'_of_commutes_span_blockProjection
  • MPSTensor.isBlockDiagonal'_of_commutes_reindexed_wordSpan
  • MPSTensor.offBlock_zero_of_commutes_reindexed_wordSpan
Lemma 20.160Conditional Route B block split from BNT selector words

Let \(A\) be in canonical-form/BNT, assume \(1 {\lt} L\) and \(N \ge L + 1\), and suppose length-\(S\) block-selector words are given. If every assembled periodic-chain ground vector has a boundary matrix representation commuting with all length-\((1 + S)\) assembled words, then

\[ \mathcal G_{N,L}\bigl(\operatorname {Assemble}(\mu , A)\bigr) \subseteq \bigsqcup _j \mathcal G_{N,L}(A_j). \]

Together with the forward inclusion, this gives the corresponding conditional block-splitting equality. In the parent-ground-space formulation, the same hypotheses imply containment in \(\operatorname {BNTSpan}_{N}(A)\). The selector-word hypothesis and the boundary representation remain explicit: this lemma does not derive selector existence from BNT separation, nor the wrapping/open-chain boundary representation. It is the formal composition matching the block-injective ground-space route of [ CPGSV21 , Section IV.C ] , where the regrowing argument is restricted to block-diagonal boundary conditions.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.chainGroundSpace_toTensorFromBlocks_le_iSup_of_bntSelectorWords
  • MPSTensor.chainGroundSpace_toTensorFromBlocks_eq_iSup_of_bntSelectorWords
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianGroundSpace_le_bntSpan_of_bntSelectorWords
Lemma 20.159Conditional Route B block split from product-word span

Let \(A\) be in canonical-form/BNT, and assume \(1{\lt}L\), \(N \ge L+1\), and \(0{\lt}m\). If the length-\(m\) product-word tuples span \(\prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C})\) and every assembled periodic-chain ground vector comes with a boundary matrix representation commuting with all length-\(m\) assembled words, then

\[ \mathcal G_{N,L}\bigl(\operatorname {Assemble}(\mu , A)\bigr) \subseteq \bigsqcup _j \mathcal G_{N,L}(A_j). \]

Combined with the forward inclusion, this gives the corresponding conditional equality. In the parent-ground-space formulation, the same hypotheses imply containment in \(\operatorname {BNTSpan}_{N}(A)\). The product-word span and boundary representation remain hypotheses here; they are not derived by this composition lemma.

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.chainGroundSpace_toTensorFromBlocks_eq_iSup_of_wordTupleSpanTop

Lemma 4.2Same MPV implies trace agreement

If \(A\) and \(B\) generate the same MPV family, then \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) = \operatorname{tr}(B^w)\) for every word \(w\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.52Zero-tail cancellation for leftover zero blocks

Let \(A\) and \(B\) have the same MPV family, each decomposed as an all-zero leftover block, called the zero-tail part in this convention, plus a nonzero part. If the leftover zero-block dimensions agree, then the nonzero parts themselves have the same MPV family, including at length zero.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.17Same state gives combined-tensor MPV agreement

Assume a same-state reduction hypothesis for \((d,D)\). Let \(\mathbf{A}\) and \(\mathbf{B}\) be \(n\)-site chains with common bond dimension \(D\), with \(n \ge 3\), and assume that \(\mathbf{A}\) is injective. If \(\mathbf{A}\) and \(\mathbf{B}\) generate the same chain state, then the combined tensors of \(\mathbf{A}\) and \(\mathbf{B}\) generate the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.36Evaluation formula

\(E_\sigma (X)=\dfrac {\operatorname{tr}(\sigma X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\sigma )}\, \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.40Fixes scalar operators

For \(c\in \mathbb {C}\), \(E_\sigma (c\, \mathbb {1})=c\, \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.43Image lies in adjoint fixed points

For all \(X\), \(E_\sigma (X)\) is fixed by \(T^*\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.39Range is scalar

For every \(X\), there exists \(c\in \mathbb {C}\) with \(E_\sigma (X)=c\, \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.35Sector basis pre-matching gives basis-span equality

A sector basis pre-matching between \(P\) and \(Q\) implies equality, for every system size, of the finite-length MPV spans of the two sector bases.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.32Sector-block normality for periodic tensors

For a periodic tensor equipped with a cyclic sector decomposition, each nonzero blocked sector tensor is normal.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.110Projection step from sector fixed-point-algebra rigidity

Assume sector fixed-point-algebra rigidity. If \(X\) is an orthogonal projection supported on \(P_k\) and fixed by \(T^m\), then \(T(X)\) is again an orthogonal projection.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.34Sector-match propagation under translation

A single gauge-phase match between blocked sectors propagates to all cyclic translates of that sector pair.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.35Per-site proportionality from blocked sector match

If all aligned blocked sectors match up to gauge and phase, then the original tensors are related by repeated-block proportionality.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.15Similarity is an involution

For any invertible \(C \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and any linear map \(E\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the similarity transforms by \(C\) and \(C^{-1}\) compose to the identity:

\[ X \; \mapsto \; C\bigl(C^{-1}\, E(C\, X\, C^\dagger )\, (C^\dagger )^{-1}\bigr)\, C^\dagger \; =\; E(X). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.14Similarity transform preserves irreducibility

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), let \(C \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be invertible (\(\det C \neq 0\)), and let \(c {\gt} 0\). Define the similarity-transformed map

\[ E'(X) \; =\; c \cdot C^{-1}\, E\! \bigl(C\, X\, C^\dagger \bigr)\, (C^\dagger )^{-1}. \]

Then \(E'\) is also irreducible.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.16Irreducibility is invariant under similarity

For any invertible \(C \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and any linear map \(E\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the similarity transform \(X \mapsto C^{-1}\, E(C\, X\, C^\dagger )\, (C^\dagger )^{-1}\) is irreducible if and only if \(E\) is.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.93Intertwining of Stinespring isometries

Let \(K : \{ 0,\ldots ,r-1\} \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(L : \{ 0,\ldots ,s-1\} \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be two Kraus families. Write \(K\mathbin {+\! +}L\) for their concatenation as a family indexed by \(\{ 0,\ldots ,r+s-1\} \). Then

\[ V_K = (\mathbb {1}_D \otimes C_{r,s}) V_{K + \! + L}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.82Entry formula for the Stinespring isometry

For indices \(i,k \in \{ 0,\dots ,D-1\} \) and \(j \in \{ 0,\dots ,r-1\} \),

\[ V_{(i,j),k} = (K_j)_{ik}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.18Dyson-series summability from factorial majorant

Assume a factorial majorant of Dyson–Phillips iterates:

\[ \| D_n(t)\| \le M\, \frac{(t\, \| L'-L\| \, M)^n}{n!}. \]

Then the Dyson series is summable in operator norm.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.53Support projection annihilates the kernel

Let \(\rho \succeq 0\) on \(\mathbb {C}^D\) with support projection \(P = \mathrm{supp}(\rho )\). For every \(v \in \mathbb {C}^D\), if \(\rho \, v = 0\), then \(P\, v = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.12Aligned gauges force proportionality – Lemma 2 of [ MGRPG+18 ]

Let \(A_1, A_2\) and \(B_1, B_2\) be injective MPS tensors with the same bond dimension \(D\). Suppose that for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (on the internal bond), all \(Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (on the external bond), and all physical indices \(i,j\),

\begin{align} \operatorname{tr}(A_1^{i}\, X\, A_2^{j}) & = \operatorname{tr}(B_1^{i}\, X\, B_2^{j}) \label{eq:internal_bond}\\ \operatorname{tr}(A_2^{j}\, Y\, A_1^{i}) & = \operatorname{tr}(B_2^{j}\, Y\, B_1^{i}) \label{eq:external_bond} \end{align}

Diagrammatically, Equation ?? is the equality obtained by inserting \(X\) on the bond between the two sites,

while Equation ?? is the corresponding equality with the insertion \(Y\) on the outer trace bond,

so the two hypotheses distinguish the internal and external virtual loops of the two-site periodic chain.

Then there exists a nonzero scalar \(\lambda \in \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) such that

\[ A_1^{i} = \lambda \, B_1^{i} \qquad \text{and}\qquad A_2^{j} = \lambda ^{-1}\, B_2^{j} \]

for all physical indices \(i, j\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.6Trace expansion over matrix units

For any linear endomorphism \(T\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the operator trace can be expanded as

\[ \operatorname{Tr}(T) = \sum _{p,q = 0}^{D-1} (T(E_{pq}))_{pq}, \]

where \(E_{pq}\) is the matrix with \(1\) in position \((p,q)\) and \(0\) elsewhere.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 6.9Partial trace preserves Hermiticity

If \(\rho _{ABC}\) is Hermitian, then \(\operatorname{tr}_A(\rho _{ABC})\), \(\operatorname{tr}_C(\rho _{ABC})\), and \(\operatorname{tr}_{AC}(\rho _{ABC})\) are all Hermitian. The same holds for bipartite partial traces \(\operatorname{tr}_A(\rho _{AB})\) and \(\operatorname{tr}_B(\rho _{AB})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.19Trace pairing for Kraus map and adjoint map

For all \(X,Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl(X\, E(Y)\bigr) = \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl(E^*(X)\, Y\bigr). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.36Transfer map of the conjugate-transposed Kraus family

For an MPS tensor \(A\), the transfer map of the conjugate-transposed Kraus family \(\{ (A^i)^\dagger \} \) is the adjoint of \(\mathcal{E}_A\) with respect to the Frobenius inner product.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.110Symmetric-projection structure of transported ES local terms

The Euclidean local projector \(P_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\) is a symmetric projection, and every transported local term \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}\) on the \(N\)-site chain is a symmetric projection.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentInteractionES_isSymmetricProjection
  • MPSTensor.localTermES_isSymmetricProjection
Lemma 20.114Non-overlap positivity for transported ES local terms

Let \(L\le N\) and let \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}},h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}\) be transported local terms whose cyclic \(L\)-windows are site-disjoint: no site of the periodic chain has cyclic offset \({\lt}L\) from both \(i\) and \(j\). Then the terms commute pointwise: for every vector \(v\),

\[ h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v) = h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v), \]

and their ordered cross term is nonnegative:

\[ 0\le \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v, h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle . \]
LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.CyclicWindowsDisjoint
  • MPSTensor.CyclicWindowsDisjoint.symm
  • MPSTensor.CyclicWindowsDisjoint.of_not_cyclicWindowsOverlap
  • MPSTensor.localTermES_commute_of_cyclic_windows_disjoint
  • MPSTensor.localTermES_re_inner_nonneg_of_cyclic_windows_disjoint
Lemma 20.109Positivity of the transported ES Hamiltonian

Each transported local term \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}\) is positive, hence the full transported parent Hamiltonian \(H_{N,\mathrm{ES}}\) is positive.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.localTermES_isPositive
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_isPositive
Lemma 11.134One-sector-per-block decomposition preserves the represented family

The one-sector-per-block sector decomposition associated to \((\mu _k,B_k)_k\) represents the same MPS family as the original weighted block sum \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.5Functoriality of twisting

The twisted tensor satisfies the composition law

\[ \widetilde{A}_{gh} = \widetilde{(\widetilde{A}_h)}_g, \]

i.e. twisting by \(gh\) is the same as first twisting by \(h\) and then by \(g\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.6Identity twist

Twisting by the identity group element is trivial: \(\widetilde{A}_1 = A\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.44Twisted transfer as a mixed transfer operator

The twisted transfer map of \(A\) is the mixed transfer operator of the pair \((A,B_u)\):

\[ \mathcal{E}_u = F_{A,B_u}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.52Same-witness identities from compared wrapped witnesses

Fix a dummy physical letter \(\eta \). Suppose the wrapped and mirror one-sided witnesses \(Y^+\) and \(Y^-\) have been extracted. If, for every middle word \(\mu \), the witnesses agree on the backgrounds built from \(\eta \),

\[ Y^+_{\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )} = Y^-_{\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )}, \]

then there is a single family \(Y_\mu \) satisfying

\[ A^\mu A^b X = Y_\mu A^b, \qquad X A^a A^\mu = A^a Y_\mu \]

for all letters \(a,b\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.40Unitary Kraus mixing

If \(u\) is unitary, then replacing Kraus operators \(\{ A_i\} \) by their unitary mixtures \(\{ \sum _j u_{ij} A_j\} \) preserves the channel: \(\sum _i (\sum _j u_{ij} A_j)\, Y\, (\sum _j u_{ij} A_j)^\dagger = \sum _i A_i\, Y\, A_i^\dagger \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.21Vector span from matrix span

If \(T_N(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(\varphi \neq 0\), then \(K_N(A, \varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.11Virtual bond gauge – 3-site case

Let \(A = (A_0, A_1, A_2)\) and \(B = (B_0, B_1, B_2)\) be two 3-site injective periodic chains with common bond dimension \(D \ge 1\) and \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(B)\) (same MPV family). Then at the middle bond there exists an invertible matrix \(Z \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) such that the virtual-insertion trace coefficients agree after conjugation:

\[ \operatorname{tr}(A_0^{i_0} X\, A_1^{i_1} A_2^{i_2}) = \operatorname{tr}(B_0^{i_0} Z^{-1} X Z\, B_1^{i_1} B_2^{i_2}) \]

for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and all physical indices \(i_0, i_1, i_2\).

In [ MGRPG+18 , Lemma 1 ] , the statement is given for arbitrary chain length \(n \ge 3\) and any bond position, with uniqueness of \(Z\) up to scalar. The chain-level generalization to arbitrary \(n \ge 3\) and any bond position is not pursued here.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.25Cumulative Wielandt analysis

Let \(A\) be a normal MPS tensor with bond dimension \(D \ge 1\). Then the following auxiliary consequences hold simultaneously:

  1. \(T_{D^2}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (cumulative span stabilises);

  2. there exists a word \(w_0\) with \(|w_0| \le D^2\) and \(\operatorname{tr}(A^{w_0}) \ne 0\);

  3. there exist \(w_0, \mu \ne 0, \varphi \ne 0\) with \(|w_0| \le D^2\) and \(A^{w_0} \varphi = \mu \, \varphi \);

  4. for every \(\varphi \ne 0\), \(K_{D^2}(A, \varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.66Amplifying fixed-length word commutation

If a boundary matrix \(X\) commutes with every word product of length \(m\), then it commutes with every word product whose length is a multiple \(q\, m\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.50Chunking: word span embeds into blocked word span

\(S_{nL}(A) \subseteq S_n(A^{[L]})\) for all \(n, L\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.43Word span products

\(S_m(A) \cdot S_n(A) \subseteq S_{m+n}(A)\): the product of word spans at lengths \(m\) and \(n\) is contained in the word span at length \(m + n\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.9Word trace as entrywise inner product

For a word \(\sigma \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N\),

\[ \operatorname{tr}\bigl(A^\sigma (B^\sigma )^\dagger \bigr) = \sum _{j,k = 0}^{D-1} (A^\sigma )_{jk}\, \overline{(B^\sigma )_{jk}}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.7Word span characterises block injectivity

\(S_L(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) if and only if \(A\) is \(L\)-block injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 20.51Middle-word backgrounds for the two wrapped complements

Fix a dummy physical letter \(\eta \) and a middle word \(\mu \) of length \(N-(L_0+1)\). Write \(\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )\) for the last-site wrapped background and \(\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )\) for the opposite wrapped background. Their exposed complements are both exactly \(\mu \).

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.wrappedMiddleBackground
  • MPSTensor.mirrorMiddleBackground
  • MPSTensor.wrappedMiddleBackground_complement
  • MPSTensor.mirrorMiddleBackground_complement
Lemma 20.48Wrapped-window one-sided compatibility

Assume \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block injective with \(L_0{\gt}0\) and \(M \ge L_0\). If the reduced wrapped window at the last site has boundary matrices \(Y_\tau \), then for every physical letter \(j\) one has

\[ C^+_\tau A^j X = Y_\tau A^j, \]

where \(C^+_\tau \) is the complementary word seen by that wrapped window.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.17Diagonal \(Z\)-gauge transports diagonal weights

Under \(\nu _i\neq 0\) for all \(i\),

\[ Z\, \operatorname{diag}(\nu )=\operatorname{diag}(\mu ). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.16Diagonal \(Z\)-gauge has finite order under matched powers

If \(\mu _i^m=\nu _i^m\) and \(\nu _i\neq 0\) for all \(i\), then

\[ Z^m=\mathbb {1}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.14Ratio rescales denominator back to numerator

If \(\nu \neq 0\), then

\[ z(\mu ,\nu )\, \nu =\mu . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.13Matched powers imply root-of-unity ratio

If \(\mu ^m=\nu ^m\) and \(\nu \neq 0\), then

\[ z(\mu ,\nu )^m = 1. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.113Adjacent local kernels realize the intersection property

If \(A\) is injective and \(L\ge 2\), then on an \((L+1)\)-site chain the kernels of the two adjacent transported \(L\)-site local terms are exactly the transported \((L+1)\)-site MPS ground space:

\[ h_{0,\mathrm{ES}}v=0\ \text{ and }\ h_{1,\mathrm{ES}}v=0 \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad v\in G_{L+1}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A). \]
LaTeX
Lean
